full_response:
We start from the ordinary Schwarzschild metric written in the double-null (“Eddington–Finkelstein’’) coordinates  

u = t − r_* , v = t + r_* ,  with r_* = r + 2GM \ln\!\left|\frac{r}{2GM}-1\right| ,

so that, suppressing the angular pieces,  

ds² = −(1 − 2GM/r)\,du\,dv.  (1)

1.  Express e^{(v-u)/(4GM)} in terms of r  

v − u = 2 r_*  ⇒   
e^{(v-u)/(4GM)} = e^{r_*/(2GM)}
               = e^{r/(2GM)}\Bigl(\frac{r}{2GM}-1\Bigr).  (2)

Hence  

\frac{r}{2GM}-1 = e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}.  (3)

2.  Solve (3) for (1 − 2GM/r)  

1 - \frac{2GM}{r}
      = \frac{r-2GM}{r}
      = \frac{2GM}{r}\Bigl(\frac{r}{2GM}-1\Bigr)  
      = \frac{2GM}{r}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}\,e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}.  (4)

The prefactor  

f(r) ≡ \frac{2GM}{r}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}  

is a smooth, non–vanishing function at the Schwarzschild radius r = R_S ≡ 2GM
(indeed f(2GM)=e^{-1}).

3.  Substitute (4) into the t–r part (1) of the metric:

ds² = −f(r)\,e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}\,du\,dv  
     = -\frac{2GM}{r}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}\,e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}\,du\,dv + r² dΩ².  (5)

Equation (5) replaces the troublesome factor (1 − 2GM/r) by the product of
the innocuous f(r) and the exponential e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}, setting the stage for the
Kruskal–Szekeres coordinates.

\boxed{1-\dfrac{2GM}{r}= \left(\dfrac{2GM}{r}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}\right)\,e^{(v-u)/(4GM)},\quad
ds^{2}_{t\!-\!r}= -\dfrac{2GM}{r}\,e^{-r/(2GM)}\,e^{(v-u)/(4GM)}\,du\,dv}

Confidence: 74%
